idea-plugin.name: Warm Burnout
idea-plugin.description:
The theme for developers who are already burned out but still have deadlines.

Mostly warm palette, one cool type accent, contrast-audited to WCAG standards. Full UI theme: editor, sidebar, tabs, toolbar, popups, everything.

  • Dark: AAA contrast (7:1+) on every syntax token. For the 3am sessions.
  • Light: AA contrast (4.5:1+) on warm sepia cream. For when someone opens the blinds.

Bold keywords, italic types, readable comments. The style system adds non-color cues for code structure when the warm palette starts to blur together.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.2.0: Classic non-Islands variants are back. Pick Warm Burnout Dark or Warm Burnout Light if Islands chrome causes performance or redraw issues for you (see IJPL-222923). Comprehensive per-language coverage for ~60 language plugins (Bash, Dart, Go, Haml, JS, Kotlin, Lua, PHP, Python, Rust, Ruby, Swift, TS, XML, YAML, etc.) so identifiers no longer fall through to teal/blue defaults. Compose composable function calls render in the function color. Semantic-highlighting and bracket rainbow palettes collapsed to five on-brand warm tones. Toolwindow stripe button hover and selected states have visible contrast against the panel. Tame bold TODO highlight, Rider/C# attribute coverage, Islands popup/tooltip backgrounds.

1.0.2: Dropped classic variants. Islands themes only. Fixed toolbar borders and project gradient.

1.0.1: Fix editor color scheme not loading when selecting the UI theme.

1.0.0: Initial release.
